dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Anne. The village is falling apart. Tell me what you know.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: More than I'd like. People are sick. Some are dead. The rest are too scared to talk about it.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Sarah, up at the lab, was working on something. A cure, she called it. Then she stopped coming by for supplies.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: You think she's still alive?

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Alive, maybe. Well, no. Word is she got too close to her own samples. The lab's locked tight, and the key... let's just say it ended up buried with someone who shouldn't have had it.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: The graveyard.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Mm. And George at the powerstation can tell you why the lab has no lights. You'll need both, if you're serious about this.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: George. The lab's dark. I need it running.

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Blown fuse. Substation line to the lab.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Spare?

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: No. Last one's inside the lab itself. On a shelf, near the breaker panel.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Of course it is.

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Bring it here. I'll switch the line over. Don't touch the panel yourself.

dialogue_content.speaker: marcus
dialogue_content.line: Hold it. State your business.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Sarah's sample. She left one here. I need it to finish her cure.

dialogue_content.speaker: marcus
dialogue_content.line: Back shelf. Cold storage. Don't drop it.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Sarah's infected. The lab has power now. I can finish what she started.

dialogue_content.speaker: marcus
dialogue_content.line: Then move. I'll hold this floor. If you get the cure working, this place becomes worth defending.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: You shouldn't be in here. The samples... I couldn't contain it. I'm symptomatic.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I know. I brought the last sample from the hospital. Power's back on.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: Then listen carefully. The synthesizer is calibrated already. Load the sample, run cycle three. It will produce a single dose.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: And then I use it on you.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: Logically, yes. I'm the one who can refine it further once I'm lucid. Don't waste it on sentiment — waste it on utility.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Understood. Sit tight.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Sarah, something's off. This outbreak — it doesn't feel random anymore.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: I've been thinking the same. The strain patterns I'm seeing... they're too consistent. Engineered, possibly.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Engineered by who?

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: I don't know. But someone at the outpost has been making demands of Anne at the store. She might know more than she lets on.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Viktor?

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: Viktor's the muscle. There's a doctor with him — clinical, methodical. That's who I'd be watching. Start with Anne. She hears everything.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Anne. I need to know who's been giving Viktor's people orders.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Straight to it, then. Alright. A man comes through here every so often — soft-spoken, gloved, never buys anything useful. Asks questions, though. About Sarah. About the lab.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: The doctor.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: That's what Viktor's men call him. He's not running errands for Viktor — it's the other way around, if you ask me.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Where do I find him?

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Deepest part of the outpost. You'll need a key — the militia keep a spare in the cave. And George at the powerstation has been complaining someone keeps siphoning power toward that outpost. Talk to him. He'll want a word.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: George. Anne said you've been seeing odd draws on the grid.

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Not odd. Deliberate. Someone ran a hard line to the outpost. Bypassed my meters.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Could you trace it?

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Already did. Terminates in the lower outpost. Whatever they're powering, it's drawing more than the village does.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I'm going in. Anything I should know?

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: The outpost's locked. Spare key's in the cave — militia stash. Go. I'll keep the lights on here.

dialogue_content.speaker: viktor
dialogue_content.line: You shouldn't be here. Turn around and I'll pretend I didn't see you.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I'm not here for you, Viktor. I'm here for the man you've been taking orders from.

dialogue_content.speaker: viktor
dialogue_content.line: I take orders from no one.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Then explain the cable running into your lower level. The shipments. The questions about Sarah.

dialogue_content.speaker: viktor
dialogue_content.line: ...Careful what you accuse me of.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I'm not accusing. I'm passing through. Whatever he promised you — it ends today.

dialogue_content.speaker: viktor
dialogue_content.line: Then go. See for yourself what you're walking into. Don't come back through here expecting the same courtesy.

dialogue_content.speaker: the_doctor
dialogue_content.line: Ah. The variable arrives. I wondered when you'd find your way down here.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: You did this. The outbreak. All of it.

dialogue_content.speaker: the_doctor
dialogue_content.line: I refined it. The pathogen existed — I simply gave it direction. Purpose. Do you understand what we're witnessing? A complete restructuring of human society in real time.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: People are dying.

dialogue_content.speaker: the_doctor
dialogue_content.line: People die regardless. What I am producing will outlast them. Outlast you. Outlast Viktor and his little kingdom.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Not if I take your research with me.

dialogue_content.speaker: the_doctor
dialogue_content.line: You won't. But by all means — try. The toolkit is on the bench. I won't stop you taking it. I'm curious what you'll do with it.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: It was the doctor. He didn't even hide it. He's been engineering the strain from inside the outpost.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: Then we have our answer. And Viktor?

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: A puppet who thinks he's holding the strings. He let me walk past him.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: That won't last. Once he understands what the doctor really is, he'll either kill him or protect him. Either way, we don't have long.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I brought the toolkit. We'll need it for the boat. And I've got wood — enough to fortify this place properly.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: Do it. If we lose the lab, we lose the cure. Fortify here, then find me a sample. Whatever he's growing down there — that's where we'll start.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: The cure's done what it can here. There's talk of a boat at the dock.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: Mary's vessel. Broken, last I heard. If she can be made seaworthy, that's our window off the island.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: I've got a toolkit and wood. I'll see what she needs.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: Good. Take the work in order: boat, then provisions, then passengers. Don't get sentimental about the sequence.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Understood. Hold the lab until I send word.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: I'll be here. Bring the boat home.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: You the one Sarah sent? Hope you brought more than good intentions.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Toolkit. Wood. Enough to patch her, I think.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: Then stop talking and start working. Hull's split along the starboard side. Fix that, she floats.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: And after?

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: Anyone who wants out, gets out. That's the deal. No politics, no passengers I have to argue with.

dialogue_content.speaker: linda
dialogue_content.line: Didn't expect company today.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Mary's boat is fixed. We're getting people off the island. I need food for the crossing.

dialogue_content.speaker: linda
dialogue_content.line: Crates by the barn. Been saving them for a reason I couldn't name. Maybe this was it.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Come with us, Linda. There's room.

dialogue_content.speaker: linda
dialogue_content.line: Land's mine. I'll stay quiet, like always. Take the food. Take it and go.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: You've got that look. Something's moving.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Boat's fixed. We're leaving on the next tide. You should come.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Me? On a boat? Wonders never cease.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Pass the word to anyone you trust. Dock, soon as they can make it.

dialogue_content.speaker: anne
dialogue_content.line: Already done it in my head. I'll close up and head down. Don't leave without me.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Wouldn't dream of it.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: George. We've got a boat. Leaving tonight.

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Grid's still running.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Grid won't matter if there's no one left to use it.

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Fair point. I'll lock down what I can. Meet you at the dock.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Don't be late. Tide doesn't wait.

dialogue_content.speaker: george
dialogue_content.line: Neither do I.

dialogue_content.speaker: marcus
dialogue_content.line: You shouldn't be up here.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Boat's fixed. We've got food. We're leaving.

dialogue_content.speaker: marcus
dialogue_content.line: Viktor's still out there. He'll come for the dock the moment he hears an engine.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Then we don't give him time. Hospital's fortified — leave it to the dead and come with us.

dialogue_content.speaker: marcus
dialogue_content.line: Hmph. Always hated boats. Lead on.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Boat's seaworthy. Food's loaded. Marcus, George, Anne — all coming.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: And Viktor?

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: We sail before he knows we're gone. The lab needs to hold until we're clear of the harbour. Fortify the door behind us.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: Logical. Hand me the wood — I'll set the brace once you're out.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Pack your notes. The cure leaves with us. The mainland needs what's in your head more than this island ever did.

dialogue_content.speaker: sarah
dialogue_content.line: Then let's not waste the tide.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: She's floating. Engine's purring. We waiting on anyone?

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Marcus, George, Anne, Sarah. They're behind me. Linda stayed.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: Her choice. Always is.

dialogue_content.speaker: PLAYER
dialogue_content.line: Cast off the moment the last of them's aboard. Don't wait for shouting from the road.

dialogue_content.speaker: mary
dialogue_content.line: Wasn't planning to. Get on. We're going home — wherever that turns out to be.
